User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the Eritas Gateway application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the user entity.

signUpDate.description: Date and time when the user signed up.

lastLogin.description: Date and time of the user's last login.

profilePicture.description: Url to the user's profile picture.

definition.description: Stores user profile information. Uses path-based ownership for simplicity and security. This path structure isolates user data, avoiding the need for `get()` calls in rules and enabling QAPs (rules are not filters).

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user, corresponding to their Firebase Auth UID.

firestore.reasoning:
Based on the provided entities and application requirements, the primary data structure revolves around user management. The structure prioritizes authorization independence and supports Query Atomic Properties (QAPs) by segregating user data based on ownership. The structure is designed to provide easy scalability and robust security rules.

The core entity is `User`, stored under `/users/{userId}`. This path-based ownership model immediately establishes clear ownership for user-specific data and simplifies security rules.

This structure avoids `get()` calls in security rules, crucial for authorization independence. Each user's data is self-contained, allowing atomic operations (transactions/batches) without relying on external data.

QAPs are supported naturally by this segregation. Listing operations are inherently limited to the specific user's data. No filtering is required in the rules, as each collection is homogeneous in its security posture.
